title: 60 Day Internal Task Tracker
content_md:
# 60 Day Internal Task Tracker

- Code: ffcdad1e
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=ffcdad1e7132415db7ae05c55f92b4e2
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/b79d7b9ec9c04b0b934c3d83ee585aa7?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 6m 53s
- Resources:
  - [Caleb's First 60 Days Tracker](None)

transcript.text: All right, so this is our first 60 days plan we follow this for 80 to 90 percent of clients
There's some clients that are
Unique where you know, maybe they have a large site
Maybe they're in a highly highly competitive space like personal injury, but for the most part
90 plus percent of clients. This is what we're going to do in the first 60 days
So the focus of this is on three specific things number one obviously actually getting results
So we wanted to make sure that everyone knew exactly what we need to do over the first 60 days because that's by far the most
Complicated once we get past the first 60 days
What we do every month is just additional content additional links
Over and over again, but the first 60 days much more complex. We want to make sure we get results
We want to make sure it happens quickly
So that's the first goal of this the second goal is to overcome buyers remorse
This is a well-known psychological issue where when somebody buys something expensive
The first feeling they often get is remorse
So you'll see at the beginning of this the first week or two
There's a lot of front-loaded communications to overcome that buyers remorse
the third focus area is to
Make the client happy to
Pay the third payment right so the first payment is before month one the second payment is before month two
The third payment is before month three typically
Obviously for this to be kicked off. We'll have collected the first payment then the second payment very rare. We don't get that
It's only been a month in
So no no issues usually getting that but by the time the third payment is coming around
We've been working with them for a couple of months. We might have some results. We might not
Usually the best results we see are at the end of month three
So if they make that third payment by the end of month three
The rank map is going to look really good and they're likely going to be a long-term client
But the rank map might not look significantly different at the end of the second month
So that's the third aspect
So you'll see what we're doing as we approach that end of second month to try to get it
So I'm not going to walk through every single one of these but just keep in mind those those goals
So obviously right after they close we're going to send them a doc you sign update the high level all the CRM
Everything like that we'll send the invoice nothing else happens until that happens. Okay
Then they're going to get an email sequence. So the the person that they're on the sales call with is going to send them an email
I
will reply to that
Or automated to for me to reply to that and then the operations manager will also
Reply to it and the ops manager is going to give all the access that we need along with loom videos and walk-throughs
On how to actually get that it'll also include a questionnaire link
30-day timeline exactly what they can expect in the next 30 days now
We want to make sure that they actually grant access
So we'll create a task once this is sent to remind them. Hey, you know, we need the access
Then we're going to send them a handwritten thank you note
This is one of the things that we do to overcome buyers remorse the way we handle this logistically. I bought like 60
Thank-you notes. I filled all of them out sent them to the sales staff
They filled all of them out sent them to the ops manager
She filled all of them out and that way they're ready to go when the client closes
We can just write the ops manager just writes the client name on one and drops it in the mailbox
Okay, then we basically start doing audit work. We're delivering this internal this initial rank assessment within a few days
Then we're going to do the GBP audit
Again, we do need access if we don't have it yet
questionnaire reminder, so we want that then
We're going to do the landing page audit. What does their landing page look like?
To make sure that the service entities are mapped. So basically just getting all of these audits done
okay, so then we're going to do the first meeting and
The goal of that meeting we're going to walk through the GBP audit the landing page audit and the content gap analysis
So we're also going to talk through what the service entity list is the content calendar approval of changes things like that
I'll change this to service list because we don't use keywords
So the client approves that we start to make changes
Yep, we just
talk through that meeting and
After that meeting then boom we go ahead. We start making changes fix and AP consistency
This is just basically making all of the changes we just agreed to we'll record a loom and send that over to the client saying
Hey, we fixed everything. So this is fairly quick, right?
So all of the tech fixes are done within the first month and the client's going to get a loom saying look at all this stuff
We did to fix everything. Okay
Also, we're going to start the content sprint. So these happen in parallel, right?
We're going to start the tech fixes and the content sprint at the same time these activities happen simultaneous
So we're going to start publishing content the quantity depends on the price point for the client build the gvp post plan
And yeah, we'll source the links all of this then
At the end of the first month, we're going to pull the leads math lead snap rank data
Compare against the baseline prepare the month one report send them that report with a loom video
And send them a meeting invite for the monthly review. This is not something we do every month
We pretty much only do it at the end of month one
So we'll hold that first monthly review call and then we're going to send this swag box
This is you know, they've just had the first month the review call
They see all of the tech stuff that we fixed all the content we published
They see a path forward what we're going to do in month two then they get a swag box
This is all to build up the goodwill to make sure we make it into that third month
Where we're actually going to see crazy results for most clients
Then we're going to do month
Two work call it retention check in we're going to have that call right before the month two payment
Here is
If we don't see any results if things are pretty much exactly the same. We'll typically wave
The third and or the fourth and fifth potentially fees and then ongoing from there
So this is our 60 day first 60 day sprint exactly what we do how we manage new clients
That you guys would find this useful
